hard.next_step: 50% confidence 06 JUL - Second deep dive completed, Susan advises she will be in contact this week, next step is supposedly RFI between two vendors

north_star_verdict.headline: Live two-vendor RFI (Zycus vs Coupa) with Susan Innes actively championing after two tailored deep-dive demos (9 Jun CLM, 25 Jun sourcing/supplier); the next gates are reaching the unmet budget owner and arming Susan with a quantified ROI before Coupa competes on price.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 20 May 2026, close 30 Oct 2026 (116 days out). In Formal Evaluation since 22 Jun (14 days), qualified 22 May. Last buyer touch 30 Jun (incoming email) and rep-logged CPO meeting 1 Jul. Motion (discovery 1 Jun, CLM demo 9 Jun, sourcing demo 25 Jun, pricing requested 18 Jun, RFI shortlist confirmed 6 Jul) is on pace for a ~5-month cycle; close date credible if the economic buyer engages within 30 days.
evidence: Zycus is one of two vendors in Civeo's RFI: Next_Step__c 6 Jul 2026 states 'next step is supposedly RFI between two vendors', and Coupa is the named rival ('Has mentioned also receiving demo and information from Coupa', Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026).

Susan Innes (General Manager Supply Chain, PRIMARY contact/Evaluator) is a strong active champion: she scoped and hosted both deep-dive demos, requested ballpark pricing (18 Jun), and confirmed on 6 Jul she will follow up this week on RFI next steps (Next_Step__c 6 Jul 2026).

Broad in-person engagement: the 25 Jun Brisbane demo drew Civeo's supply-chain leadership team, Susan Innes, Harvinder Dulku (Procurement Manager), Tyler Scott (supply chain operations), Lananh (commercial finance), plus category and projects managers (Avoma 'Zycus Demo - Procurement', 25 Jun 2026).

Acute, well-articulated pain but not yet quantified: Civeo runs paper-based contracts in an O drive, tracks expiries in Excel, and legal has no workflow visibility (prior 1 Jun 2026 discovery, Susan Innes); no ROI figure exists yet.

No economic buyer is engaged: Susan said budget sign-off depends on her selling it internally ('if I can sell it', 1 Jun 2026), and Zycus APAC head Carl Kimball only opened the purchase-process conversation with Civeo's CPO on 1 Jul (Task 1 Jul 2026).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is confirmed and vivid but not yet quantified. On the 1 Jun 2026 discovery call Susan Innes (GM Supply Chain) described Civeo as 'starting from ground zero', with contracts in an O drive, expiry tracking in manual Excel, certifications in an unsupported safety system, and legal counsel with no pipeline or workflow visibility. The pain owners are Susan and Iona Borthwick (General Counsel and Company Secretary). No dollar baseline exists yet: no contract-cycle time, no legal-hours-saved estimate, and no compliance cost-avoidance figure, even though Susan requested ballpark pricing on 18 Jun (Next_Step__c). Because budget sits above her, a quantified value case is the missing piece; co-building an ROI one-pager with Susan is the ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged or mapped. Susan Innes explicitly signalled she does not hold final budget authority on the 1 Jun 2026 call ('if I can sell it', and 'having the two of us on the platform helps me get the economy of scale and the return on investment'), so sign-off rests with a CFO/COO and the corporate-mandate sponsor implied by the AIS reason 'Corp mandate'. The one budget-authority signal this sweep is the 1 Jul 2026 task noting Civeo's CPO met Zycus APAC head Carl Kimball to discuss the purchase process, which points to a CPO above Susan but names no Salesforce contact. Because the org is buying under a corporate mandate, identifying and engaging that budget owner is the highest-leverage MEDDPICC move before the RFI closes.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from the demo dialogue rather than a formal scoring sheet. On the 25 Jun 2026 sourcing/supplier demo the team probed configurability of supplier onboarding, category-to-master-data mapping (Max, Tyler), duplicate-supplier prevention at ABN level (Harvinder Dulku), Dun and Bradstreet / EFT bank-detail integration, and Merlin AI supplier-risk scanning of public sources. Lananh (commercial finance) pressed hard on item-level and non-conforming-bid pricing analysis for complex tenders, and the ICT and legal teams were flagged as reviewers of security and integration (Susan, 11 and 18 Jun 2026 emails). Confirmed criteria: configurable onboarding, supplier risk/compliance automation, sourcing-event workflow with tiered approvals, and integration to ERP/finance. Inferred/open: the item-level pricing-analysis fit that Lananh raised and the security posture ICT must sign off.
sources: Avoma 'Zycus Demo - Procurement' 25 Jun 2026 (Lananh, Harvinder Dulku, Max, Tyler Scott)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured early-funnel evaluation: discovery (1 Jun) then two tailored deep-dive demos (CLM 9 Jun, sourcing/supplier 25 Jun), a pricing request (18 Jun), and now an RFI down to two vendors (Susan, 6 Jul). Susan is orchestrating internally: on 29 Jun she said she 'haven't had a chance to circle back to my team post Thursday's session' and would gather feedback and send an update early the following week, showing a team-consensus step before advancing. Above her, Zycus APAC head Carl Kimball met Civeo's CPO on 1 Jul to walk the purchase process, indicating the formal approval chain runs Susan (GM Supply Chain) up to a CPO/executive under a corporate mandate. The approval mechanics beyond that (who signs, budget threshold) are not yet documented and should be captured in writing.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown at this early stage and largely premature; Civeo remains in discovery/evaluation (Susan: 'we are only in discovery phase at this point', 10 Jun 2026). Susan flagged that the legal team and ICT team would review Zycus material (11 and 18 Jun 2026 emails), and Iona Borthwick (General Counsel and Company Secretary) is the contracting owner once selection happens. Civeo's probity rules already surfaced as a live process constraint: Susan declined the Zycus Horizon Singapore invitation citing 'internal probity re...' (28 Jun 2026), so an active evaluation-integrity policy governs vendor engagement and will shape the paper trail. Visibility improves once the RFI outcome is known and legal/ICT begin their review; capturing Civeo's procurement and legal review steps in writing is the next step.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed, operational, and owner-attributed. Civeo has essentially no procurement system: paper-based contracts in an O drive, manual Excel expiry tracking, certifications in an unsupported safety system, and manual supplier onboarding via a PDF registration pack with heavy re-keying (Susan Innes and team, 1 Jun and 25 Jun 2026). On the 25 Jun demo Harvinder Dulku described chasing supplier certificates and insurances manually, Tyler and Susan detailed backdoor 'one-time vendor' and 'Scanman' spend with no visibility ('you find out down the track you've got invoice spend to a 100,000 with someone you didn't even know'), and the team confirmed 1,100 active suppliers on the books with no automated compliance monitoring. The pain owners are Susan (supply chain governance) and Harvinder (contracts/supplier compliance). This is a strong, acute driver; the gap is converting it to a dollar figure (see Metrics).
sources: Avoma 'Zycus Demo - Procurement' 25 Jun 2026 (Harvinder Dulku, Tyler Scott, Susan Innes)

champion.narrative: Susan Innes (General Manager Supply Chain, PRIMARY / Evaluator) is a strong, active champion. She restarted the evaluation after joining Civeo, scoped and hosted both deep-dive demos (CLM 9 Jun, sourcing/supplier 25 Jun), requested ballpark pricing (18 Jun), and confirmed on 6 Jul she will drive the RFI next steps this week. She carries prior Zycus experience from Stanwell and Aurizon (Next_Step__c 16 Jun 2026), which shortens trust-building and lets her validate fit rather than learn cold. Her advocacy shows in access: she brought legal (Iona Borthwick), IT (Tyler Scott), procurement (Harvinder Dulku), finance (Lananh) and category managers into the room, and she personally coordinates internal feedback. The one weakness that keeps this from a pure strength is authority: she does not hold budget ('if I can sell it', 1 Jun) and is currently our single strong thread, so pairing her with an engaged economic buyer is what converts advocacy into a signature.

competition.narrative: This is a two-horse RFI: Coupa versus Zycus (Next_Step__c 6 Jul 2026, 'RFI between two vendors'; Coupa first named 18 Jun 2026). No other active rival appears across calls, fields, or tasks this sweep. The incumbent state is manual/paper-based (no real system), so do-nothing is a weak threat given acute pain and a corporate mandate. Bonfire is a settled negative: Civeo Canada uses it but Australia canceled its unused Bonfire licence after a non-collaborative pick (Susan, 1 Jun 2026), which Zycus should use to argue for Australia-Canada alignment rather than treat as a live rival. The wedge on Coupa is Susan's prior Zycus trust plus Merlin AI depth (agentic supplier onboarding, supplier-risk scanning, sourcing analytics) shown on 25 Jun; the exposure is price and the unresolved item-level pricing-analysis question Lananh raised.
sources: Avoma discovery call 1 Jun 2026 (Susan Innes, Bonfire)

deal_movement.summary: Deal is live and progressing on an early-funnel motion. Since qualification (22 May) it has moved through discovery (1 Jun), a CLM demo (9 Jun), a pricing request (18 Jun), an in-person sourcing/supplier demo (25 Jun), a rep-executive CPO meeting (1 Jul), and an RFI down-select to two vendors (6 Jul). Last genuine two-way buyer touch was Susan's 30 Jun email; the 1 Jul CPO/Carl Kimball meeting is a rep-side executive touch awaiting buyer follow-up.

items.change: Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation
In-person sourcing and supplier-management deep-dive demo held at Civeo Brisbane office with supply-chain leadership team
Zycus APAC head Carl Kimball met Civeo's CPO to discuss the purchase process
Buyer confirmed next step is an RFI between two vendors (Zycus vs Coupa)

competitive_position.summary: Two-vendor RFI: Coupa is the single active current threat (named 18 Jun, RFI confirmed 6 Jul) and ranks highest by current evidence. The incumbent state is manual/paper-based (do-nothing, low threat given acute pain and a corporate mandate). Bonfire is a declined/dormant alternative: used by Civeo Canada but canceled in Australia after a non-collaborative pick, so it is history, not a live rival. Zycus holds a trust edge (Susan's prior Zycus use at Stanwell/Aurizon) and AI depth; the exposure vs Coupa is price and the unresolved item-level pricing-analysis fit Lananh raised on 25 Jun.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Has mentioned also receiving demo and information from Coupa. Next step is supposedly RFI between two vendors.
competitors.how_we_win: Leverage Susan's prior Zycus trust and position Merlin AI (agentic supplier onboarding, supplier-risk scanning, sourcing analytics) as the differentiator; resolve Lananh's item-level/non-conforming pricing-analysis question and hand Susan a quantified ROI so she can defend value over a lower Coupa price.

competitors.name: Manual / do-nothing
competitors.quote: We're starting from ground zero. Everything's paper based, contracts in an O drive, manual Excel tracking for expiries, certifications in an unsupported safety system.
competitors.how_we_win: Pain is acute and there is a corporate mandate, so inertia is weak; keep urgency high by tying automation to compliance risk and the 1,100-supplier manual-onboarding load.

competitors.name: Bonfire
competitors.quote: Australia had it licensed but no one used it when I got here. We paid two years worth of Bonfire that no one even picked up, so I canceled it.
competitors.how_we_win: Bonfire is out in Australia; use the split-decision history to push for Australia-Canada alignment so Civeo does not repeat a non-collaborative pick.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on configurable supplier onboarding, supplier-risk/compliance automation, and AI (Merlin), which landed well on the 25 Jun demo. The open fit question is complex item-level and non-conforming-bid pricing analysis for large tenders, raised by Lananh, plus ICT security/integration sign-off.

best_practice_check.summary: Two-vendor RFI against Coupa with a strong champion but no engaged budget owner and no quantified value case. The win levers are ROI, reaching the economic buyer, and global alignment.
flags: Hand Susan a quantified ROI one-pager (cycle-time, legal hours, compliance risk) so she can defend value upward against a likely lower Coupa price.
Map and engage the economic buyer now: Susan holds no budget, and the 1 Jul CPO/Carl Kimball meeting is the opening to reach the corporate-mandate sponsor.
Manage Australia-Canada alignment to avoid repeating the Bonfire split-decision, using the shared-platform economy-of-scale argument Susan already raised.

AIS is Moderate (5) on a corporate mandate; the AI story is a genuine differentiator vs Coupa and should anchor the RFI positioning.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5 / Moderate, reason 'Corp mandate'. Calls corroborate real AI appetite: the buyer engaged positively with Merlin agentic onboarding and risk scanning on 25 Jun. Not overtly AI-hungry but receptive and mandate-driven.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of two vendors in Civeo's RFI with a strong, Zycus-experienced champion in Susan Innes, but the win hinges on reaching the unmet budget owner and quantifying ROI before Coupa competes on price.

win_position.text: Zycus is one of two RFI finalists with Susan Innes (GM Supply Chain) actively championing after two tailored deep-dive demos (9 Jun CLM, 25 Jun sourcing), and her prior Zycus use at Stanwell/Aurizon gives a real trust edge (Next_Step 6 Jul 2026).

No economic buyer is engaged: Susan said budget rides on her selling it internally ('if I can sell it', 1 Jun 2026), and the CPO has only been touched via Carl Kimball's 1 Jul purchase-process meeting.
Coupa is the live rival in the same RFI (Next_Step 18 Jun 2026) and no quantified ROI exists yet, leaving Zycus exposed if Coupa competes on price.

deal_momentum.text: Fast cadence: discovery 1 Jun, CLM demo 9 Jun, ballpark pricing requested 18 Jun, in-person sourcing demo 25 Jun, and RFI down-select to two vendors confirmed 6 Jul 2026.
Zycus APAC head Carl Kimball met Civeo's CPO on 1 Jul 2026 to walk the purchase process, lifting the conversation toward the budget owner.
Momentum now waits on Susan's internal loop: on 29 Jun she still had to circle back to her team for post-demo feedback before advancing.

customer_commitment.text: Civeo hosted Zycus in person at its Brisbane office on 25 Jun for a two-hour sourcing/supplier demo with 10-plus leadership-team stakeholders across procurement, finance, IT and ops.
Commitment is still discovery-stage: Susan said 'we are only in discovery phase at this point' (10 Jun 2026) and probity rules blocked the Horizon Singapore invite (28 Jun 2026).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on Susan, who holds no budget; no CFO/COO/CPO economic buyer is mapped or engaged by the deal team.
Two-horse RFI against Coupa with no quantified value case, so a lower Coupa price could decide it on cost.
Australia-Canada split-decision history (Bonfire canceled after a non-collaborative pick) could resurface without global alignment.

executive_alignment.evidence: Economic buyer unmapped and unengaged; Susan holds no budget ('if I can sell it', 1 Jun 2026).

champion.evidence: Susan Innes is a strong active champion with prior Zycus experience driving the RFI (Next_Step 6 Jul 2026).

scope_change.detail: Scope unchanged this sweep; full S2C suite plus Merlin AI remains in evaluation across CLM, sourcing and supplier management.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive and Zycus-experienced champion, deep Zycus-specific questions and a pricing request, but still a two-vendor RFI with no explicit favourable comparison against Coupa (Next_Step 6 Jul; Avoma 25 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Acute qualitative pain (paper-based, no system, 1,100 suppliers onboarded manually, compliance risk) but no quantified ROI or savings figure yet; ballpark pricing only requested 18 Jun.

gaps: Economic buyer / budget owner not named in Salesforce (corporate-mandate CPO only touched via a rep-executive meeting).
No quantified ROI / business case documented yet.
